symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden transient expressive/receptive language difficulty after cognitive exertion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertion-triggered language crash on a known post-viral background fits a neurocognitive crash.
Isolated aphasia is a recognised focal cortical (usually dominant-hemisphere) symptom of TIA/stroke and must be taken seriously.
A negative FAST reduces but does not exclude stroke/TIA — isolated aphasia can occur with an otherwise negative FAST.

symptoms.symptom_name: Stereotyped recurrent pattern with the same cognitive-exertion trigger

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A consistent prior pattern with the same trigger strongly supports a recurrent crash over a new vascular event.
Recurrent identical stereotyped episodes are less typical of TIA; a first/atypical episode would argue the other way.
Continuous preserved awareness without automatisms argues against a focal seizure.
Gradual easing with rest fits a crash; progressive/persistent focal deficit would demand emergency assessment.
Normal hydration and no asterixis argue against metabolic encephalopathy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact cranial nerves, no arm drift; MMSE attention/recall impaired (examination)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Impaired attention/recall with intact focal neurology fits a functional cognitive crash.
Absence of focal motor/sensory deficits argues against a completed stroke.
Established post-viral cognitive phenotype supports a recurrent crash.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Sudden language difficulty after cognitive exertion, easing with rest, FAST-negative and matching a stereotyped recurrent pattern, most likely reflects a post-viral neurocognitive crash — but isolated aphasia is a focal cortical symptom, so TIA/stroke cannot be excluded remotely; a first/atypical episode must be treated as TIA and urgent in-person assessment is required.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Key remote step; positive features or a new/atypical episode mandate 000.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent in-person neurological assessment (± brain imaging/carotid work-up if TIA suspected)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Isolated aphasia warrants same-day assessment to exclude TIA, especially if first/atypical.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Cognitive assessment and blood screen (once TIA excluded)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To characterise the post-viral cognitive phenotype and exclude reversible contributors.
